metadata.revision_note: Updated from cand_cross_domain_unsupervised after idea_debate synthesis round 2. Rate-distortion theory elevated to co-primary. Unsupervised detection de-prioritized. New tasks: multi-L0 confound decomposition, CMI estimation, geometric constant, lateral inhibition, ITAC-CMI bridge, cross-domain CMI, bifurcation analysis.
metadata.key_lesson_from_iter5: Must use Gemma 2 2B (not GPT-2 Small); EDA=SAEBench metric; proxy labels inflate AUROC; ITAC needs real activations; within-width null is a primary finding; architecture must be covariate in GAM

tasks.name: Environment Setup and Model Download
tasks.description: Install dependencies (sae-lens>=4.0, transformer-lens, torch>=2.1, scikit-learn, scipy, pygam). Download Gemma 2 2B model and Gemma Scope SAEs: L10/L12/L20 at 16k/65k widths (residual stream). For L12 16k, download MULTIPLE L0 configs: L0={22, 42, 82, 163} for multi-L0 decomposition. Verify SAELens loads each config via SAE.from_pretrained(). Smoke test: encode 100 tokens, verify non-zero activations, measure VRAM.

pilot.pass_criteria: SAELens loads all SAE configs without error; smoke test produces non-zero activations for >=90% of tokens; VRAM usage < 20GB on single GPU; all 4 L0 configs for L12-16k load successfully

tasks.name: First-Letter Absorption Baseline (Improved, 50+ words/letter)
tasks.description: Reproduce and IMPROVE Chanin et al. first-letter absorption on Gemma 2 2B + Gemma Scope L12 16k. KEY IMPROVEMENT: use 50+ words per letter (up from 25 in pilot) for better probe quality. Train k-sparse (k=5) logistic regression probes per letter. Apply probe quality gate (F1>0.85). Measure absorption rate with bootstrap 95% CI. Run on L12-16k and L12-65k. CRITICAL: calibrate controls -- pilot showed C1=9.2% (random, too high) and C2=59.5% (shuffled, way too high). Investigate and fix control implementation before proceeding. Also run dense probe ceiling (C3) and untrained SAE control (C4 if feasible).

pilot.pass_criteria: Absorption rate within 0.5x-3x of published 15-35%; probe F1 > 0.85 for at least 20/26 letters (improved from 4/25 in pilot); random control < 2%; shuffled control < 20% (investigate if higher)

visualizations.description: Per-letter absorption rates with bootstrap 95% CI
First-letter validation with controls and probe quality per letter

tasks.name: Cross-Domain Absorption: City -> Country
tasks.description: Primary cross-domain test for H1. Measure absorption on city-country hierarchy using Gemma 2 2B + Gemma Scope L12 16k and 65k. Use RAVEL-sourced single-token cities. Apply probe quality gate. Exclude countries with <5 cities or F1<0.50. Stratify by probe quality tier (F1>0.85 vs 0.70-0.85). Run threshold sensitivity (5x4 grid). Include shuffled country control.

pilot.pass_criteria: Absorption rate measurable for at least 10 countries; probe F1>0.85 for at least 15/20 countries; shuffled control < 10%; threshold sensitivity CV < 0.5

visualizations.description: City-country absorption rate per country with CI
Threshold sensitivity grid (cosine x magnitude gap)

tasks.name: Cross-Domain Absorption: City -> Continent
tasks.description: Coarser hierarchy for H1. Measure absorption on city-continent (6 continents) on Gemma 2 2B L12 16k. Fewer parent classes but larger child sets per parent -- pilot showed 6.5% rate. Include shuffled control. Report per-continent and aggregate rates.

pilot.pass_criteria: Probe F1 > 0.85 for at least 4/6 continents; absorption rate measurable; shuffled control < 10%

visualizations.description: City-continent absorption rate per continent with CI

tasks.name: Cross-Domain Absorption: City -> Language
tasks.description: Different hierarchy type for H1. Measure absorption on city-language mapping (10+ languages) on Gemma 2 2B L12 16k. Pilot showed 6.6% rate. Include shuffled control. Report per-language and aggregate.

pilot.pass_criteria: Probe F1 > 0.85 for at least 7/10 languages; absorption rate measurable; shuffled control < 10%

visualizations.description: City-language absorption rate per language with CI

tasks.name: Cross-Domain Absorption: Animal -> Class
tasks.description: Non-geographic domain for H1. Measure absorption on animal-class (mammal/bird/reptile/fish/insect) on Gemma 2 2B L12 16k. Uses WordNet hierarchy. Pilot showed only 1.4% rate. Include shuffled control.

pilot.pass_criteria: Probe F1 > 0.85 for at least 3/5 classes; absorption rate measurable; shuffled control < 10%

visualizations.description: Animal-class absorption rate per class with CI

tasks.name: Cross-Domain Comparative Analysis and Hierarchy Predictors (H6)
tasks.description: Aggregate all cross-domain results. Compute hierarchy predictors (co-occurrence frequency ratio, fan-out, depth, parent frequency) from 1M-token corpus. Correlate predictors with absorption rate (Spearman, Bonferroni-corrected). Fit linear model: absorption_rate ~ co_occurrence_ratio + fan_out + parent_frequency + L0. Perform FDR correction. Report domain-dependent absorption rates as primary empirical finding.

pilot.pass_criteria: All predictor correlations computed; at least 4 domains have measurable absorption rates; FDR correction applied

visualizations.description: Cross-domain absorption summary: domain x rate x predictors x controls
Absorption rate vs hierarchy predictors across domains

tasks.name: Multi-L0 Confound Decomposition (H2 Primary)
tasks.description: PRIMARY CONTRIBUTION. Decompose false negatives at L0={22, 42, 82, 163} on first-letter task into three components: (a) hedging (recoverable at different L0), (b) reconstruction error (residual norm > 2 sigma), (c) hierarchy-driven (persistent across L0, low reconstruction error). Pilot showed 96.9% hierarchy-driven at L0=22. NOVEL: profile how the decomposition changes across L0, testing prediction that hierarchy-driven fraction peaks at optimal L0 and decreases at extremes.

pilot.pass_criteria: Decomposition computed at all 4 L0 values; hierarchy-driven > 80% at L0=22 (replicating pilot); clear trend across L0 values; total false negatives >50 at each L0 for statistical power

visualizations.description: Multi-L0 confound decomposition: hedging vs reconstruction error vs hierarchy-driven
Confound decomposition per L0

tasks.name: Confound Control: Partial Correlations and Rosenbaum Matching
tasks.description: Compute partial correlations between absorption rate and 4 quality metrics (SP-F1, SCR, TPP, Unlearning) controlling for log(L0), log(width), arch_class across all Gemma Scope SAEs. Apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction. Rosenbaum sensitivity: both Mahalanobis (cross-width) and within-width matching. Report Gamma for both. Within-width null (Gamma~1.0) is an expected positive finding.

pilot.pass_criteria: Partial correlations computed for all 4 metrics; FDR correction applied; Rosenbaum Gamma computed for both matching strategies

visualizations.description: Partial correlations: metric x raw_r x partial_r x FDR_p

tasks.name: Ablation: Absorption Threshold Sensitivity (5x4 Grid)
tasks.description: Full 5x4 threshold grid on first-letter: cosine={0.01, 0.02, 0.025, 0.03, 0.05} x gap={0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0}. Report CV across thresholds, Kendall tau rank stability. CV < 0.3 = stable; CV > 0.5 = brittle. This is the FIRST systematic threshold sensitivity analysis of the Chanin absorption metric.

pilot.pass_criteria: All 20 grid cells computed; CV reported; Kendall tau rank stability computed; absorption rates monotonic in expected direction

visualizations.description: Threshold sensitivity: absorption rate across cosine x gap grid

tasks.name: CMI Estimation: Rate-Distortion Diagnostic (H3 Primary)
tasks.description: PRIMARY NOVEL CONTRIBUTION. For all 25 first-letter features on Gemma 2 2B L12 16k: compute I(X; w_parent | f_child) using k-NN MI estimator (Kraskov et al., 2004) in decoder-direction subspace at d'={10, 20, 30, 50}. Correlate CMI with observed absorption rate. Target: Spearman rho < -0.3. Letters with low CMI should be preferentially absorbed (rate-distortion optimal). Report Mann-Whitney U test for absorbed vs non-absorbed partition, Cohen's d. This is the FIRST application of successive refinement theorem to SAE absorption.

pilot.pass_criteria: CMI estimated for at least 20/25 letters without numerical instability; CMI values non-negative and finite; Mann-Whitney p < 0.05; Cohen's d > 0.5; Spearman rho < -0.2 at best subspace dimension

visualizations.description: CMI vs absorption rate per letter (25 points, with regression line and CI band)
CMI distribution: absorbed vs non-absorbed letters
CMI-absorption correlation stability across subspace dimensions d'={10,20,30,50}

tasks.name: ITAC-CMI Connection: Theory-Practice Bridge
tasks.description: Validate that ITAC is a scalar proxy for CMI. Compute both ITAC (Var ratio on real activations, 100k tokens) and CMI (k-NN estimator) for the same 25 first-letter features. Report Spearman correlation between ITAC and CMI. Target: rho > 0.5. This bridges the information-theoretic diagnostic (CMI) with the practical metric (ITAC), establishing that ITAC tracks the successive refinement quantity.

pilot.pass_criteria: Both ITAC and CMI computed for at least 20/25 letters; Spearman correlation between ITAC and CMI computed; no NaN values

visualizations.description: ITAC vs CMI per letter (25 points, with regression line)

tasks.name: Geometric Constant Validation: Decoder Weight Analysis
tasks.description: Compute c(w_P, w_C) = ||w_P||^2 * (1 - cos^2(w_P, w_C)) from SAE decoder weights (sae.W_dec) for all first-letter parent-child pairs. Test whether c modulates the absorption threshold beyond CMI alone. Specifically: does CMI/c(w_P, w_C) correlate more strongly with absorption rate than raw CMI? This is a zero-GPU computation (decoder weights only) that validates the geometric component of the rate-distortion theory.
tasks.comment_on_time: Pure decoder weight computation, no GPU needed for forward pass. Requires loading SAE weights only.

pilot.pass_criteria: c(w_P, w_C) computed for all 25 letter pairs; CMI/c correlation with absorption computed; comparison with raw CMI correlation reported

visualizations.description: CMI/c vs absorption rate (25 letters), compared with raw CMI

tasks.name: Lateral Inhibition Check: Per-Token False-Negative Prediction
tasks.description: For 100k tokens on Gemma 2 2B L12 16k: compute inhibition ratio q = G_PC * a_C / (theta_P - b_P) per token, where G_PC = decoder weight interaction term, a_C = child activation, theta_P = JumpReLU threshold for parent. Test whether q > 1 predicts per-token false negatives. Target: precision@50 > 0.3. This validates the mechanistic lateral inhibition hypothesis from LCA dynamics.

pilot.pass_criteria: Inhibition ratio q computed for at least 10k tokens; precision@50 for q>1 predictor > 0.2; ROC-AUC > 0.55 for per-token prediction

visualizations.description: Inhibition ratio q vs parent false-negative indicator per token
Per-token false-negative prediction using inhibition ratio q

tasks.name: Lateral Inhibition Bifurcation: JumpReLU vs L1 (H7)
tasks.description: Compare absorption patterns between JumpReLU SAEs (Gemma Scope, hard threshold) and L1+ReLU SAEs (soft threshold). PREDICTION: JumpReLU shows bimodal per-parent absorption distribution (binary: all-or-nothing), while L1 shows continuous distribution. Use Gemma Scope JumpReLU SAEs at multiple L0 values. For L1 comparison: check if SAELens has L1-trained SAEs for Gemma 2 2B (gpt2-small-res-jb as fallback reference). KS test for distribution difference. If L1 SAEs unavailable for Gemma 2 2B, train a small custom L1 SAE (~1 hour) or use GPT-2 Small L1 SAEs as cross-model reference with appropriate caveats.
tasks.comment_on_time: May need up to 60 min if training custom L1 SAE. If using existing SAEs only, ~20 min.

pilot.pass_criteria: At least 2 JumpReLU and 1 L1 SAE compared; per-parent absorption distributions computed; KS test p-value reported; bimodality index computed for both distributions

visualizations.description: Per-parent absorption rate distributions: JumpReLU vs L1 (overlaid)
Bifurcation analysis: architecture x mean_rate x bimodality_index x KS_p

tasks.name: Phase Transition Prediction from Rate-Distortion Theory
tasks.description: From the rate-distortion framework, compute the predicted critical L0 value at which absorption becomes optimal: L0_crit = lambda / (CMI * c(w_P, w_C)). Compare with empirically observed L0 ~ 7-14 phase transition from scaling surface. This validates the theory's quantitative predictive power. Requires both CMI estimates and scaling surface data.
tasks.comment_on_time: Pure computation on existing data, no GPU needed.

pilot.pass_criteria: Predicted L0_crit computed for at least 15 letters; comparison with empirical transition point reported; relative error < 50% is encouraging

visualizations.description: Predicted vs observed critical L0 per letter

tasks.name: Cross-Domain CMI Extension
tasks.description: CONDITIONAL on H3 being supported (rho < -0.3 on first-letter). Extend CMI computation to city-continent and city-language hierarchies. Test whether CMI predicts cross-domain absorption rate differences. This bridges the rate-distortion theory (Stage 3) with the empirical characterization (Stage 1). If H3 is NOT supported, skip this task and report limitation.

pilot.pass_criteria: CMI estimated for city-continent (6 parents) and city-language (10+ parents); cross-domain CMI-absorption correlation computed

visualizations.description: CMI vs absorption rate across all domains (first-letter + cross-domain)

tasks.name: Scaling Surface: Width x L0 x Absorption Rate (H5)
tasks.description: Measure absorption rate across all available Gemma Scope SAE configurations. Fit Beta-GAM with architecture covariate (CRITICAL fix: iter5 had 360 standard + 54 JumpReLU + 6 unknown, architecture was omitted). Report interaction p-value, explained deviance. Within-architecture subset analysis. Phase transition detection. Cross-domain scaling comparison if sufficient data.
tasks.comment_on_time: May approach 60 min if many SAE configs; can be parallelized by layer.

pilot.pass_criteria: GAM converges; width-L0 interaction p < 0.05; explained deviance > 0.5; architecture covariate included; within-architecture p-value reported

visualizations.description: Absorption rate scaling surface (width x L0)
Absorption rate vs L0 at fixed widths (phase transition detection)

tasks.name: Ablation: Probe Sparsity (k=1,3,5,10)
tasks.description: Test how probe granularity affects absorption measurement. Run first-letter absorption with k={1,3,5,10} sparse probes on Gemma 2 2B L12 16k. Report absorption rate vs k. Determines robustness to probe design choices.

pilot.pass_criteria: All k values produce valid absorption rates; rates vary by <50% across k values

visualizations.description: Absorption rate vs probe sparsity k

tasks.name: Decoder Geometry Analysis (Unsupervised Pipeline, Secondary)
tasks.description: SECONDARY. Compute pairwise cosine similarity matrix for Gemma Scope L12 16k decoder (16k x 16k). Conditional cosine for pairs with global cosine > 0.1. Firing rate asymmetry filter (100k tokens). Hierarchical clustering dendrogram. Output: ranked candidate absorption pairs.

pilot.pass_criteria: Cosine matrix computed without OOM; >= 100 candidate pairs; conditional cosine distribution shows clear tail

visualizations.description: Global vs conditional cosine similarity distributions

tasks.name: Unsupervised Pipeline Validation (H4, Secondary)
tasks.description: SECONDARY. Validate unsupervised pipeline (conditional cosine + firing rate + ITAC) against first-letter gold standard. Report Spearman rho, AUROC, Precision@50. Ablate each component. PRE-REGISTERED: if rho < 0.3, report as negative result and do not deploy cross-domain. Try larger corpus (500k tokens) and stricter candidate filtering per pilot recommendations.

pilot.pass_criteria: Pipeline rho computed; AUROC computed; component ablation completed; pre-registered decision applied (rho < 0.3 = negative result)

visualizations.description: Unsupervised detection ROC (full pipeline + components)
Unsupervised validation: rho, AUROC, Prec@50 per component

decision_gates.condition: Controls not calibrated: shuffled > 20% after refinement
decision_gates.action: Investigate control implementation before proceeding to cross-domain. Check probe implementation, label shuffling procedure, threshold sensitivity.

decision_gates.condition: Absorption rate differs >3x from published 15-35% range
decision_gates.action: Debug: check SAELens version, layer choice, SAE config, probe implementation. Do NOT proceed until resolved.

decision_gates.condition: Spearman rho > -0.2 (CMI does not predict absorption direction)
decision_gates.action: De-prioritize rate-distortion framing. Skip cross_domain_cmi and phase_transition_prediction. Pivot emphasis to confound decomposition (H2) and cross-domain characterization (H1).

decision_gates.condition: All cross-domain absorption rates < 3% after probe gating and control calibration
decision_gates.action: H1 FALSIFIED. Report as informative finding (absorption is domain-specific). Domain-dependent rates are still a novel contribution. Focus on first-letter for remaining analyses.
H4 FALSIFIED. Report as negative result. Do not deploy unsupervised pipeline cross-domain. This is the expected outcome based on pilot evidence.

decision_gates.overall_scoring: H1+H2+H3 supported = strong paper (rate-distortion + cross-domain + decomposition). H1+H2 only = moderate paper (cross-domain + decomposition). H3 only = pivot to cand_rate_distortion_theory (theory-first paper). None = pivot to cand_decomposition_reframing (metric validation / negative results).

controls.name: Random probe control
controls.description: Replace trained probe with random direction; expected absorption ~0%. Pilot showed 9.2% -- must investigate.

controls.name: Shuffled label control
controls.description: Random label assignment; expected absorption ~0%. Pilot showed 59.5% on first-letter -- MUST investigate and calibrate.

controls.name: Dense probe upper bound
controls.description: LR probe on raw model activations (no SAE); ceiling for SAE-based methods. Pilot: 0.617 mean F1.

controls.name: Untrained SAE control
controls.description: Randomly initialized SAE decoder weights; expected absorption ~0%.

execution_order.note: Both depend only on setup_env; can run in parallel on separate GPUs. first_letter_improved is GATE for all subsequent tasks.
All 4 cross-domain tasks can run in parallel on separate GPUs after first_letter_improved completes.
CMI, lateral inhibition, and bifurcation can run in parallel. All depend on first_letter_improved. This is the HIGHEST PRIORITY phase after baseline.
All depend on first_letter_improved. Can run in parallel.
geometric_constant and itac_cmi_bridge depend on cmi_estimation. scaling_surface depends on first_letter_improved only.
cross_domain_analysis waits for all Phase 2 tasks. phase_transition_prediction waits for Phase 5. cross_domain_cmi is CONDITIONAL on H3 (rho < -0.3). unsupervised_validation is secondary.
